questions.question: Solve for x: |2x - 5| = |x + 3|
questions.explanation: Set up two cases: (2x - 5) = (x + 3) gives x = 8; and (2x - 5) = -(x + 3) gives 3x = 2, so x = 2/3. Check both in the original equation.

questions.question: Find all values of x that satisfy |3x - 7| < 5.
questions.explanation: Rewrite as -5 < 3x - 7 < 5. Add 7 to all parts: 2 < 3x < 12. Divide by 3: 2/3 < x < 4.

questions.question: A manufacturing process produces bolts with length specified as |L - 10| ≤ 0.2 mm, where L is the measured length in mm. What is the range of acceptable lengths?
questions.explanation: The inequality |L - 10| ≤ 0.2 means -0.2 ≤ L - 10 ≤ 0.2. Add 10 to all parts to get 9.8 ≤ L ≤ 10.2.

questions.question: Solve: |x - 4| + |x + 2| = 8
questions.explanation: Test critical points x = 4 and x = -2 to determine sign regions. For x ≥ 4: (x - 4) + (x + 2) = 8 gives x = 5. For x ≤ -2: -(x - 4) - (x + 2) = 8 gives x = -3. Check that -2 < x < 4 yields no solution.

questions.question: For what values of k does |2x - 3| = k have exactly one solution?
questions.explanation: The absolute value equation |2x - 3| = k has exactly one solution when k = 0 (the expression equals zero at a single point x = 3/2). For any k > 0, there are two solutions.

questions.question: Solve the compound inequality: |x + 1| ≥ 3 and |x - 2| < 4
questions.explanation: |x + 1| ≥ 3 gives x ≤ -4 or x ≥ 2. |x - 2| < 4 gives -2 < x < 6. The intersection is (-4 < x ≤ -4) or (2 ≤ x < 6), which simplifies to x = -4 or 2 ≤ x < 6. (Note: If strict inequality intended at -4, answer is 2 ≤ x < 6.)
